seoTitle: How to Enter Form 1116 in TurboTax (Foreign Tax Credit)
metaDescription: Step-by-step help entering Form 1116 in TurboTax to claim the foreign tax credit correctly, aimed at freelancers, remote workers, and expats filing in 2026.
intro: Form 1116 lets you claim a credit for tax already paid to a foreign government, which helps you avoid being taxed twice on the same income. This guide covers how to enter it in TurboTax, who typically needs it, and where filers most often go wrong.

steps.heading: Confirm you need Form 1116
steps.body: This form generally applies if you paid or accrued foreign tax on income earned abroad, such as freelance work, remote employment, or investment income from another country.

steps.heading: Gather your foreign income and tax details
steps.body: Have the amount of foreign income earned and the foreign tax paid on hand, along with the country it came from, before you start the entry.

steps.heading: Access Form 1116 in TurboTax
steps.body: Navigate to the foreign tax credit section of TurboTax, where the software will prompt you through the questions needed to populate Form 1116.

steps.heading: Enter your figures carefully
steps.body: Input your foreign income, taxes paid, and category of income as prompted. Accuracy here matters, since the form is what determines your credit amount.

steps.heading: Review common mistakes
steps.body: Double-check that you haven't mixed up income categories or overstated the credit, both of which are frequent errors that can delay processing or trigger a review.

steps.heading: Let TurboTax calculate your credit
steps.body: Once entries are complete, TurboTax will calculate the credit and apply it to reduce your overall tax liability.

faq.question: Who needs to file Form 1116?
faq.answer: Anyone who paid foreign income tax and wants to claim a credit for it, including freelancers, remote workers, and expatriates with foreign-sourced income, typically needs this form.

faq.question: Does TurboTax automate any part of Form 1116?
faq.answer: Yes, TurboTax guides you through a series of questions and handles the underlying calculations once you've entered your foreign income and tax details.

faq.question: What happens if I enter the wrong income category?
faq.answer: Misclassifying income can distort your credit calculation. Go back through the foreign tax credit interview in TurboTax to correct the category if you notice an error.

faq.question: Can small business owners with international operations use this form?
faq.answer: Yes, businesses with income or tax exposure abroad may also need to account for foreign taxes paid, and this form is where that credit gets claimed.
